data.title: Supreme Court Allows Trump to Withhold $4 Billion in Foreign Aid — WSJ
data.description:
By Lydia Wheeler

The Supreme Court on Friday granted the Trump administration's emergency request to allow it to withhold $4 billion in foreign-aid spending that had been approved by Congress.

Over the objection of the court's three liberal justices, the Supreme Court said in an order that the government had made a sufficient showing at this point that prospective beneficiaries of the aid can't sue to force it to follow appropriations laws, under the federal law that governs the president's ability to withhold funds.

The temporary order pauses a lower court decision that would force the federal government to spend the money by Sept. 30, when Congress's appropriation is set to expire. The Trump administration has been trying to avoid paying the funds by running out the clock, in a case that has ping-ponged back and forth between the Supreme Court and lower courts for months.

In a dissenting opinion, Justice Elena Kagan said the court was working in "uncharted territory" and deciding a legal question about a federal law it has only dealt with in passing.

"And, to repeat, the stakes are high: At issue is the allocation of power between the Executive and Congress over the expenditure of public moneys," she wrote separately in an explanation for her dissent that Justices Sonia Sotomayor and Ketanji Brown Jackson joined.

The dispute centers on whether President Trump can legally withhold billions of dollars in foreign-aid spending. The money at issue was appropriated to fund humanitarian foreign assistance programs through the State Department and the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which Trump dismantled shortly after taking office.

Trump notified Congress that while his administration would commit to spending about $6.5 billion of the funds before the appropriation lapsed at the end of the fiscal year on Sept. 30, it intended to withhold another $4 billion.

In a letter to congressional leadership, Trump said that he wanted to claw back the funds using his authority under the Impoundment Control Act, which gives the White House power to pause spending only in limited circumstances.

Democrats and some Republicans pushed back against the maneuver, saying it encroached on Congress's power and risked upending lawmakers' sensitive talks about a deal on government spending for the coming fiscal year.

The Trump administration said that, by his invoking of the ICA, Congress had 45 days to consider whether to sign off on the move — although by the time that period expires, the fiscal year will be over and the authorization will have lapsed.

A district court issued an order Sept. 3 stopping the administration from delaying payment. U.S. Solicitor John Sauer sought an emergency order from the Supreme Court after an appeals court refused to intervene. He argued the district court was wrong to interfere while Congress was considering the government's proposal to rescind the funds.

"It would be self-defeating and senseless for the Executive Branch to obligate the very funds that it is asking Congress to rescind," he told the justices in a court filing.

A coalition of groups challenging Trump's foreign-aid moves, led by the Global Health Council, urged the Supreme Court not to disturb the lower court's order. They said the government didn't properly trigger the congressional review period, which requires the House and Senate to be notified on the same day. More broadly, they contend that the president overstepped his authority by trying to hold back funding Congress had authorized.

Chief Justice John Roberts issued an interim administrative order on Sept. 9 to temporarily halt that district court ruling while the court considered Sauer's emergency request.

Write to Lydia Wheeler at lydia.wheeler@wsj.com

data.title: Sinclair, Nexstar Will Bring Kimmel Back to Their ABC Affiliates — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph De Avila

Sinclair is putting Jimmy Kimmel back on the air.

The TV broadcaster, which owns dozens of ABC affiliates in markets including Washington, D.C., Seattle and St. Louis, said it will end its pre-emption of "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" and air the late-night show again beginning Friday.

ABC suspended the show earlier this month after remarks Kimmel made about the killing of conservative activist and podcast host Charlie Kirk. ABC brought the show back Tuesday, but Sinclair said it still wouldn't carry it at that time.

"Our decision to preempt this program was independent of any government interaction or influence," Sinclair said Friday. "Free speech provides broadcasters with the right to exercise judgment as to the content on their local stations."

Nexstar Media Group, another major broadcaster that pulled Kimmel from its local ABC affiliates, said earlier this week that it continues to evaluate the show and is speaking with Disney, "with a focus on ensuring the program reflects and respects the diverse interests of the communities we serve." Nexstar didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

Last week, after Federal Communications Commission Chairman Brendan Carr suggested the agency might come down on broadcasters that didn't take action against Kimmel, Sinclair and Nexstar said they would pull the show from their local affiliates. ABC then suspended it for four days.

Sinclair had initially called for Kimmel to apologize to the Kirk family and make a personal donation to the family and Kirk's organization, Turning Point USA.

Sinclair said it has received feedback from viewers, advertisers and others since pulling "Jimmy Kimmel Live!" and "remains committed to serving our communities with programming that reflects their priorities, earns their trust, and promotes constructive dialogue."

Sinclair said it has had discussions with ABC, and proposed "measures to strengthen accountability, viewer feedback and community dialogue." Sinclair also proposed adding a network-wide independent ombudsman, it said.

Sinclair said it respects the right of ABC and its parent, Disney, to make such decisions.

ABC hasn't made any editorial or content concessions, people familiar with the matter said.

Write to Joseph De Avila at joseph.deavila@wsj.com

data.title: Seneca Bancorp, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Seneca Bancorp, Inc., a community-oriented savings and loan institution, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its ongoing efforts to serve its market area through residential mortgage and commercial loans.

Financial Highlights

Total interest income: Seneca Bancorp reported a total interest income of $3.595 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, an increase from $3.336 million in the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by higher interest income from loans and securities.

Net interest income: The company's net interest income for the three months ended June 30, 2025, was $2.231 million, compared to $1.971 million in the same period in 2024. This indicates improved interest income after accounting for interest expenses.

Net income (loss): Seneca Bancorp experienced a net loss of $(196) thousand for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net income of $176 thousand in the same period in 2024, highlighting a shift to a net loss.

Net income (loss) per common share - basic: The net loss per common share was $(0.11) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net income of $0.10 per share in the same period in 2024.

Net income (loss) per common share - diluted: Similarly, the net loss per common share on a diluted basis was $(0.11) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net income of $0.10 per share in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Seneca Bancorp primarily generates revenue through its community-oriented savings and loan institution, focusing on residential mortgage and commercial loans. The business model involves accepting deposits from customers within its market area and investing those funds.

Geographical Performance: The company operates its executive offices and main branch in Baldwinsville, New York, with additional branches in Liverpool, North Syracuse, Manlius, and Bridgeport, New York. Seneca Bancorp serves customers primarily within these regions.

Sales Units: As of June 30, 2025, the company reported a total of $209,851 thousand in loans, with a significant portion allocated to mortgage loans on real estate, including one-to-four-family first lien residential, residential construction, home equity loans, and commercial loans.

New Production Launches: The company is in the process of constructing a new branch and making improvements to existing branches, with construction in progress valued at $5,229 thousand as of June 30, 2025.

Future Outlook: Seneca Bancorp is undergoing a 'second step' conversion from a mutual holding company structure to a stock holding company structure, expected to be completed in the fourth quarter of 2025, subject to regulatory and stockholder approvals. This conversion includes plans to rename the bank to 'Seneca Savings Bank, National Association.'

SEC Filing: Seneca Bancorp, Inc. [ SNNF ] - 10-Q - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: Tower Announces AGM Results and Grant of Equity Incentives
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - September 26, 2025) - Tower Resources Ltd.  ("Tower" or the "Company") is pleased to announce the results of its Annual General Meeting of Shareholders held today (the "Meeting"). All matters put forward to shareholders were approved, including the appointment of Davidson & Company LLP, Chartered Professional Accountants, as auditor of the Company to hold office until the next annual meeting of shareholders, at a remuneration to be fixed by the Board. Shareholders also ratified, adopted and re-approved the 10% rolling share option plan (the "Plan").

The Company also announces that it has approved, subject to acceptance by the TSX Venture Exchange, the grant of 3,000,000 stock options (the "Options") to officers, directors and consultants of Tower. The Options are non-transferable and exercisable into Common Shares until September 26, 2030, at $0.20 per Option, subject to vesting provisions, with one-third vesting on the date of grant and one-third vesting ever anniversary thereafter.

About Tower Resources

Tower is a Canadian based mineral exploration company focused on the discovery and advancement of economic mineral projects in the Americas. The Company's key exploration assets, all in B.C., are the Rabbit North orogenic gold and porphyry copper-gold project located between the New Afton copper-gold and Highland Valley copper mines in the Kamloops mining district where Tower has discovered a major orogenic gold system and with limited drilling has already intersected four significant gold zones along 1.2 km of the structural trend, the Nechako porphyry-associated gold-silver project near Artemis' Blackwater project and the More Creek epithermal gold project on the critical "red line" structural zone connecting the mineral deposits of the Golden Triangle.

On behalf of the Board of DirectorsTower Resources Ltd.

Joe Dhami, President and CEO

(778) 996-4730

www.towerresources.ca

Reader Advisory

This news release contains statements that constitute "forward-looking information," including statements regarding the plans, intentions, beliefs, and current expectations of the Company, its directors, or its officers with respect to the future business activities of the Company. The words "may," "would," "could," "will," "intend," "plan," "anticipate," "believe," "estimate," "expect," "must," "next," "propose," "new," "potential," "prospective," "target," "future," "verge," "favourable," "implications," and "ongoing," and similar expressions, as they relate to the Company or its management, are intended to identify such forward-looking information. Investors are cautioned that statements including forward-looking information are not guarantees of future business activities and involve risks and uncertainties, and that the Company's future business activities may differ materially from those described in the forward-looking information as a result of various factors, including but not limited to fluctuations in market prices, successes of the operations of the Company, continued availability of capital and financing, and general economic, market, and business conditions. There can be no assurances that such forward-looking information will prove accurate, and therefore, readers are advised to rely on their own evaluation of the risks and uncertainties. The Company does not assume any obligation to update any forward-looking information except as required under the applicable securities laws.

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

data.title: Dollar Loses 0.24% to 18.3640 Mexican Pesos — Data Talk
data.description:
Dollar/Mexican peso: 18.3640 Mexican pesos per dollar (0.0545 dollar per Mexican peso)

  • This week the dollar lost 0.24% vs. the Mexican peso
  • Down for three straight weeks
  • Down 1.88% over the last three weeks
  • Largest three-week percentage decline since Friday, July 11, 2025
  • Today the dollar lost 0.62% vs. the Mexican peso
  • Largest one-day percentage decline since Thursday, Sept. 11, 2025
  • Snaps a two session winning streak
  • Off 11.92% from its 52-week high of 20.8489 hit Thursday, Jan. 16, 2025
  • Up 0.42% from its 52-week low of 18.2863 hit Tuesday, Sept. 16, 2025
  • Down 6.80% vs the Mexican peso from 52 weeks ago
  • Month-to-date it is down 1.58% vs the Mexican peso
  • Year-to-date the dollar is down 11.83% vs the Mexican peso

Data based on 5 p.m. ET values

Source: Tullett Prebon and Dow Jones Market Data

  • Today the dollar lost 0.20% vs. the Japanese yen
  • Largest one-day percentage decline since Tuesday, Sept. 16, 2025
  • Snaps a two session winning streak
  • Down four of the past six sessions
  • Off 5.59% from its 52-week high of 158.36 hit Wednesday, Jan. 8, 2025
  • Up 6.13% from its 52-week low of 140.868 hit Monday, April 21, 2025
  • Rose 5.18% vs the Japanese yen from 52 weeks ago
  • Month-to-date it is up 1.67% vs the Japanese yen
  • Year-to-date the dollar is down 4.90% vs the Japanese yen

Data based on 5 p.m. ET values

Source: Tullett Prebon and Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: Amplify Commodity Trust SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Amplify Commodity Trust, formerly known as ETF Managers Group Commodity Trust I, has released its annual 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The Trust, organized as a Delaware statutory trust, includes two series: Breakwave Dry Bulk Shipping ETF (BDRY) and Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET), both of which are commodity pools trading on the NYSE Arca. The report highlights significant changes, including a sponsor transition and the impact of geopolitical events on its operations.

Financial Highlights

Net Income: $46.77 million for the year ended June 30, 2024, resulting from net realized gains on futures contracts of $34.48 million, net unrealized gains on futures contracts of $12.90 million, and the net investment loss of $0.60 million.

Net Loss: $16.47 million for the year ended June 30, 2025, resulting from net realized losses on futures contracts of $15.93 million, net unrealized losses on investments of $0.07 million, and the net investment loss of $0.47 million.

Net Income: $46.45 million for the year ended June 30, 2024, reflecting strong performance in futures contracts despite a net investment loss.

Net Loss: $17.50 million for the year ended June 30, 2025, primarily due to significant realized and unrealized losses on futures contracts.

Business Highlights

Trust and Funds Overview: Amplify Commodity Trust, formerly known as ETF Managers Group Commodity Trust I, was organized as a Delaware statutory trust on July 23, 2014. The Trust includes two series: Breakwave Dry Bulk Shipping ETF (BDRY) and Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET), both of which are commodity pools trading on the NYSE Arca.

Sponsor Transition: Effective February 14, 2024, ETF Managers Capital LLC transferred its role as the Trust's sponsor to Amplify Investments LLC, resulting in a name change to Amplify Commodity Trust.

BDRY Investment Objective: BDRY aims to provide investors with exposure to the daily change in the price of dry bulk freight futures by tracking the performance of a portfolio consisting of exchange-cleared futures contracts on the cost of shipping dry bulk freight.

BDRY Trading Strategy: BDRY maintains long-only positions in Dry Freight Futures, with a portfolio composition of 50% Capesize, 40% Panamax, and 10% Supramax Dry Freight Futures contracts.

BWET Investment Objective: BWET seeks to provide investors with exposure to the daily change in the price of crude oil tanker freight futures by tracking a portfolio of exchange-cleared futures contracts on the cost of shipping crude oil.

BWET Trading Strategy: BWET maintains long-only positions in Oil Freight Futures, with 90% exposure in TD3C Oil Freight Futures contracts and 10% in TD20 Oil Freight Futures contracts.

Geopolitical Impact on Shipping: Geopolitical turmoil, including the Israel-Hamas conflict, has affected shipping routes, leading to disruptions across shipping segments. However, the impact has been minimized due to progress in peace talks.

Panama Canal Impact: Low water levels in the Panama Canal led to decreased vessel transits, affecting global shipping supply. Increased rainfall later improved transit levels.

Chinese Market Influence: China's demand for bulk commodities and crude oil has been a significant factor, with weaker import volumes impacting shipping rates.

Future Outlook: The crude tanker orderbook remains low, suggesting a balanced market outlook. OPEC+ production increases are expected to boost tanker demand.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The Amplify Commodity Trust, through its series Breakwave Dry Bulk Shipping ETF (BDRY) and Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET), focuses on providing investors with exposure to the daily change in the price of freight futures. The strategic initiative involves maintaining a diversified portfolio of futures contracts to track the performance of specific benchmark portfolios. BDRY aims to provide exposure to dry bulk freight futures, while BWET targets crude oil tanker freight futures. The Trust's strategic approach includes rebalancing the benchmark portfolios annually and maintaining long-only positions in futures contracts to achieve its investment objectives.

Capital Management: The Trust does not anticipate using borrowings or other lines of credit to meet its obligations. Instead, it meets liquidity needs through the sale of investments or from cash and cash equivalents. The Trust's capital management activities include the issuance and redemption of shares, with BDRY and BWET both engaging in the creation and redemption of shares to manage capital inflows and outflows. The Trust also manages expenses through a contractual expense cap, ensuring that each Fund's expenses do not exceed a specified annual rate. Additionally, the Trust holds a significant portion of its assets in futures contracts and money market funds, which are held at fair value.

Future Outlook: The Trust plans to continue its strategic focus on providing investors with exposure to freight futures, with an emphasis on maintaining a diversified and balanced portfolio. The Trust aims to manage its capital efficiently by adhering to its expense cap and ensuring liquidity through the sale of investments and cash management. The Trust's future outlook includes maintaining its investment strategy and exploring opportunities for growth within the framework of its existing series, BDRY and BWET.

Challenges and Risks

Operational Risks: The Trust and the Funds face significant operational risks due to their reliance on futures contracts for investment exposure. The change in sponsor from ETF Managers Capital LLC to Amplify Investments LLC may introduce transitional risks in management and operational processes. Additionally, geopolitical events, such as the Israel-Hamas conflict, have impacted shipping routes, potentially affecting the performance of the Funds.

Market Conditions: The Trust's performance is heavily influenced by market conditions in the shipping industry, which have been volatile due to geopolitical tensions and changes in global trade patterns. The dry bulk and crude oil tanker markets have experienced fluctuations in spot rates, impacting the Funds' returns. The Trust's reliance on futures contracts exposes it to market risks, including price volatility and liquidity risks. The management has noted the impact of geopolitical events and changes in Chinese demand on shipping rates, which could continue to affect the Funds' performance.

Regulatory Risks: The Trust faces regulatory risks due to potential changes in the regulatory environment for commodity trading, which could impact its operations and financial performance.

SEC Filing: Amplify Commodity Trust [ BDRY ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025
